name: spine-pin-diagonal
_notes: Corner-peel diagonal drag from top-right toward the spine. Drives dihedral up to roughly π/2 by ~750ms. Validates the post-#10 invariant that the spine column stays anchored at world x = 0 throughout the turn. NOTE: the trajectory assertion only exercises the harness JS reimplementation of the FLIP_VERT shader (see harness/src/bootstrap.ts → fiducialWorldPosition), so it cannot catch a regression that is purely in the inline GLSL. The pixel-min-luma assertion catches gross spine-area rendering failures, but a tight pixel-diff against a known-good baseline is the next step (see audit doc §5.1).

assertions.description: Spine-adjacent fiducial (u=0.1, v=0.5) world-space |x| stays bounded by the page-local origX (=0.1). Under the spine-tear bug the GPU shader detached this column from x=0; the JS model used here is a conservative tautology — see scenario _notes.

Spine column (canvas centre, ~3% wide x 20% tall) is showing page-cream pixels at peak dihedral, not background. Catches gross spine-region rendering failures (shader compile error, full page detachment).
